element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Torch management acts as core time/survival pressure throughout game - limited torches create soft time limit
Trial-and-error based puzzle solving with save-scumming required
Learning to recognize and avoid fatal room conditions
Using correct weapon on correct enemy at correct time
Learning monster-specific solutions and combat sequences
Three sacred artifacts combined into Staff of Ages for final boss

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Late game (Observatory, Troll Bridge, Mirror Room)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Puzzles with weight mechanics, teleportation, transformation spells
Defeating Warlock Lord and summoned Behemoth with assembled Staff

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully solving a puzzle and advancing to next room
Finding and lighting a new torch
Reading hint books that clarify objectives
typical_tension_triggers: Torch flame dying out (explicit message: 'The flame from the torch has died out')
Encountering monsters (dragon, cyclops, shark, wolf-woman, troll)
Failed puzzle attempts leading to death
Environmental hazards (being sucked into space, dissolved by slime, falling into chasm)
Complex multi-step puzzles with no clear solution
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension driven by torch depletion creating soft time limit; punctuated by sudden death states from incorrect puzzle solutions; relief comes from successful puzzle progression and resource discovery

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Books and descriptive texts contain subtle hints; NES version has dedicated hint system
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Loose stones in walls, hidden holes in ceilings/floors, removable stones, cracks in stone walls signal interactive elements; objects 'attached to wall' are non-takeable

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to torch management and basic puzzle mechanics in Entrance and Cramped Hallway; safe context for learning systems
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation to combat encounters, monster-specific solutions, moderate environmental hazards; introduction of weapons and early artifacts; trial-and-error becomes primary solving method
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex multi-step puzzles (Observatory star map, Troll Bridge toll riddles, Mirror Room weight mechanics); dangerous encounters; final assembly of Staff of Ages; approaching Warlock Lord's Cavern
notable_spikes: Troll Bridge encounter with riddle puzzle and spell management
Mirror Room bridge crossing with weight-based puzzle
Dragon Room encounter with breath attack

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple rooms explicitly noted as pitch-black or too dark to see without torch; darkness functions as both hazard and puzzle element; torch management creates resource scarcity pressure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chasm (pitch-black cave), Passageway with two arches (pitch black over there)

notable_level_patterns.description: Castle contains numerous specific death conditions tailored to room theme: dragon breath, shark attacks, cyclops mauling, acidic slime dissolution, being sucked into space, falling from heights
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Dragon Room, Shark Lake, Cyclops chamber, Charnel Room with slime, Mirror Room with weightlessness

notable_level_patterns.description: Rooms with manipulable architectural elements: removable stones, sliding star maps revealing niches, weight-sensitive bridges, stone pillars with carved shapes
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Observatory (star map), Mirror Room (bridge), Arrow Chamber, Lakmir Room

notable_level_patterns.description: Specific rooms designed around combat with particular enemies; correct weapon must be selected and used appropriately
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Troll Bridge (troll with spear and invisibility spell interaction), Dragon Room, Cyclops chamber

notable_level_patterns.description: Three sacred artifacts scattered throughout castle; collection sequence gates progress toward final boss; artifacts combine into Staff of Ages

sources_used: tcrf - Shadowgate (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor (unused messages, room names, puzzle descriptions, death conditions)
wikipedia - Shadowgate (gameplay mechanics, torch system, puzzle structure, artifact collection, plot structure)
